Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

I don't know enough about YAML to say for sure, but this might mean that the input files themselves are not generated correctly, since whitespace matters in these cases. Have you run the input through another YAML parser to verify correctness?  What generated the input?

I'm all in favor of making the input parsing more robust, but we need to be careful that this doesn't somehow bit is later if an attribute is parsed against the wrong onbject because of a bad indentation.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Given such a yaml file:
# cat 2.yaml
-  a
- bc

parse it with yq:
# yq < 2.yaml
[
  "a",
  "bc"
]

But if new_indent is set to 3 in the patched code, "bc" will be eaten to "c".
Please correct me if there is any mistake.

Patch Set 1:

Given such a yaml file:
# cat 2.yaml
-  a
- bc

Patch Set 1:

sorry I'm confused by the format of comment. The content of the file should be:
<minus><space><space>a
<minus><space>bc

Patch Set 1:

Alright I need to provide details on the internals of libyaml which is causing this issue.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

I hope the explanation makes things clear.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Builds for x86_64 centos7,rhel8.5 successful
Job output URL: https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/37480/results.html

 Commencing initial testing: runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey runtests@zfs runtests@zfs+DNE sanity-lnet@ldiskfs+DNE sanity-lnet@zfs

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> sorry I'm confused by the format of comment. The content of the file should be:
> <minus><space><space>a
> <minus><space>bc

No that is not the case. https://github.com/yaml/libyaml/issues/224
The python YAML format of YAML generates a different YAML token stream vs
the other indentation LNet sometimes uses. The different being

-    seq

   - seq

both above are valid but generate different white spacing in the libyaml internal buffer.

commit.subject: LU-10391 lnet: filter out white spaces
commit.message:
LU-10391 lnet: filter out white spaces

For the libyaml library two methods exist to construct an internal
YAML document. One is with the creation of yaml_event_t and submitting
it, yaml_emitter_emit(), to the emitter. The second method is using
some source like a file. In both cases the input is processed and
placed into an internal buffer which is passed to the read handler,
yaml_netlink_read_handler(). This buffer ends up looking in the
raw text of the configuration file passed and this includes all
the various whitespaces. Due to an internal processing bug both
creation methods don't yeild the same exact internal buffer
contents. In the sequence case for sources from a file will
contain extra white spacing. Our current Netlink implement
doesn't filter off that extra white spacing so the values packed
into the Netlink pack contains leading white spaces which is
seen as an error. The fix is to skip those extra white space if
they exist.

Change-Id: I7445ffb486d6d39c681ab4e5a85e0b835509c9ee
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Fixes: 70149f4ea89 ("LU-9680 utils: fix Netlink / YAML library handling")
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53020
Reviewed-by: Feng Lei <flei@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
